title: Migrate admin auth from localStorage to Supabase
description:
Architecture: Consolidate dual auth systems

CURRENT STATE: Two parallel admin auth systems
1. localStorage token-based (AdminAuthManager) - used by admin pages
2. Supabase profile-based (isAdmin function) - used by slug CRUD

PROBLEM: Inconsistent security model, confusing for developers

RECOMMENDATION (Option A): Full Supabase migration
- Rationale: Better security with RLS, integrated with main auth, cleaner architecture
- NOT urgent: localStorage tests are passing

TIMELINE: ~10-15 hours total
- Audit & Design: 1-2 hours
- Auth UI: 2-3 hours
- Page Migration: 3-4 hours
- Testing: 1-2 hours
- Cleanup: 1 hour

FILES TO UPDATE:
- lib/adminAuth.ts (delete)
- lib/__tests__/adminAuth.test.ts (delete)
- components/auth/admin/* (rebuild)
- redux/slices/authSlice.ts (update)
- app/api/admin/validate/route.ts (replace)
- app/api/admin/request-access/route.ts (replace)

Has admin slug management UI with E2E tests. Worktree at /Users/trilliumsmith/code/worktrees/massage/supabase-slug-migration. Still very stale but not deleted — needs proper evaluation of whether to rebase or redo.

title: Anonymous appointment booking for event pages
description: Users at events like SCaLE should be able to book without providing email, phone, or other contact info. Only first name required. Specifically needed for the scale23x display page.

title: Fix duplicate appointments via idempotent confirmation endpoint
description:
P1 Bug: Duplicate calendar events created on repeated admin confirmation

PROBLEM: /api/confirm endpoint creates new calendar event on each click
- No idempotency check
- requestId generated but never persisted in DB
- Multiple clicks = duplicate events

ROOT CAUSE: app/api/confirm/route.ts (lines 13-85)
- Only validates HMAC hash
- No duplicate detection
- No database tracking

SOLUTION: Supabase-backed appointment_requests table
- Track request status: pending → confirmed → cancelled
- Idempotency key: requestId (unique constraint)
- Audit trail: confirmed_by, confirmed_at, calendar_event_id

DATABASE:
New table: appointment_requests
- request_id (text, unique)
- status (pending/confirmed/cancelled)
- email, first_name, last_name, phone, start_time, end_time, duration
- location (jsonb), timezone
- calendar_event_id, calendar_event_link
- confirmed_by (uuid), confirmed_at (timestamptz)
- request_data (jsonb backup)
- created_at, updated_at

IMPLEMENTATION (5 phases, ~6 hours for phases 1-4):
Phase 1 (30 min): DB migration
Phase 2 (1h): Update handleAppointmentRequest.ts (insert pending)
Phase 3 (2h): Rewrite /api/confirm (idempotency + redirect)
Phase 4 (30 min): Simplify approval URLs
Phase 5 (3h future): Add Supabase auth integration

TESTING:
- First confirmation creates calendar event
- Second confirmation redirects to existing (no new event)
- Invalid requestId returns 404
- Cancelled appointments return 410
- E2E prevents duplicate appointments

READY TO IMPLEMENT: Yes (phases 1-4 can start immediately)

Cherry-pick conflicts in 4 files: ReviewSubmissionEmail.ts, contactFormEmail.ts, ClientConfirmEmail.ts, ClientRequestEmail.ts. Origin/main changed these templates (siteConfig derivation). Need to manually merge the escapeHtml boundary changes with the siteConfig template changes.
notes: Validated (Feb 24): XSS escapeHtml is NOT on main — confirmed security gap. Branch has 3 commits touching 15 files total. Cherry-pick of first commit (ba387fc) conflicts in 4 files: ReviewSubmissionEmail.ts, contactFormEmail.ts, ClientConfirmEmail.ts, ClientRequestEmail.ts. Conflicts are due to siteConfig derivation changes on main. Commits also mix in structural refactors (async signatures, eventPageUrl prop, PostHog tracking). Cleanest path: manually apply escapeHtml to main's current templates rather than cherry-picking.

title: Refactor lib/slugConfigurations/fetchSlugConfigurationData.ts (252 lines)
description:
File exceeds 250-line limit at 252 lines. Functional code should be small and single-scoped — extract logical sections into smaller modules.

If this is a config file, data file, generated code, or structural file (e.g. barrel exports, route definitions, type declarations), add it to .husky/large-files-allowlist.txt instead of refactoring.

title: PostHog feature flags: usage reference
description:
Reference guide for PostHog feature flags in project

QUICK START:
React: useFeatureFlagEnabled("flag-key") from posthog-js/react
JS: posthog.isFeatureEnabled("flag-key")

CURRENT SETUP (context/AnalyticsContext.tsx):
- Subdomain-aware keys (PROD vs DEV environments)
- Local evaluation enabled (no flicker on first load)
- Person profiles always enabled
- Proxied via /hostpog (bypasses ad-blockers)

BEST PRACTICES:
1. Wrap flags in reusable functions (lib/feature-flags.ts)
2. Handle undefined state during initial load
3. Use consistent naming (kebab-case: new-booking-flow)
4. Clean up old flags after rollout complete
5. Bootstrap flags for SSR to prevent UI flicker

COMMON USE CASES:
- Gradual rollout (10% → 25% → 50% → 100%)
- A/B testing (multiple variants)
- Kill switch for buggy features
- Beta features for specific users
- Pricing or config changes

TARGETING: By email, user properties, UTM params, custom properties

TESTING:
- Unit: Mock useFeatureFlagEnabled in Vitest
- E2E: page.evaluate(() => posthog.overrideFeatureFlag(...))
- Local: Browser console posthog.overrideFeatureFlag()

title: Live /display page with multiplayer presence for events
description:
Event-day live booking page at /display. People scan QR at the booth, see available slots on their phone, book one.

FLOW:
- QR code at booth → /display on phone
- Pick duration (5-30 min in 5-min increments)
- See available time slots for today
- See multiplayer presence ('3 people looking at 2:00 PM')
- Book a slot → confirms on calendar

SMART REFRESH (not blind polling):
- Visibility API: re-fetch when tab becomes visible
- Intersection Observer: re-fetch when slot list is in viewport
- User interaction: re-fetch on duration change, slot tap
- Minimum interval: 15-30 seconds between fetches

MULTIPLAYER PRESENCE (Supabase Realtime):
- Join presence channel on page load
- Broadcast which slot user is viewing/hovering
- Show viewer count badges on slots ('🔥 3 people looking')
- Creates urgency / social proof

TECH:
- New /display route
- Reuses existing availability pipeline (createPageConfiguration, getBusyTimes)
- Supabase Realtime Presence for multiplayer
- Duration options: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 min
- Single day view (today only)
- Mobile-first design

OPTIONAL:
- Kiosk mode variant (read-only display board at booth)
notes:
PRICING: Free + tips. No payment UI needed. No pricing display. Just slot selection and booking confirmation.

DURATIONS: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 min (5-min increments). Need to add 5, 10, 20, 25 to VALID_DURATIONS in config.ts. No pricing tiers needed since it's free.

SCOPE CONFIRMED:
- Mobile-first (QR scan from booth)
- Today only (no date picker)
- Duration picker (5-30 min)
- Live slot list with presence badges
- Smart refresh (visibility API, intersection observer, interaction)
- Supabase Realtime Presence
- No payment flow
- Booking creates Google Calendar event

title: Add 20, 25 min to VALID_DURATIONS and DEFAULT_PRICING
description: config.ts: Add 20 and 25 to VALID_DURATIONS array. Add pricing entries for 5, 10, 20, 25 in DEFAULT_PRICING (pro-rated from base price, or $0 for event mode). Two lines changed.

title: DurationPicker: hide price display for free events
description: Add optional showPrice prop (default true). When false, skip the GeneratePrice component rendering. DurationPicker already accepts allowedDurations as a prop so [5,10,15,20,25,30] just works. Low effort — one conditional around price display.

title: TimeButton: add presence badge support
description: Extend TimeButton to accept optional presenceCount prop. When > 0, render a small badge showing viewer count (e.g. '3 looking'). TimeList needs to merge slot data with presence data from Supabase hook before passing to TimeButton. Medium effort — new prop + badge UI + data merge in TimeList.

title: Create EventBookingForm component
description: Lightweight replacement for BookingForm for event /display page. Fields: first name, last name, email, phone. No Formik, no payment, no location/hotel/parking fields. Simple form that POSTs to /api/onsite/request. New component, doesn't modify existing BookingForm.

title: Create useSmartRefresh hook
description: Hook that triggers slot re-fetch based on smart triggers instead of blind polling. Triggers: (1) document.visibilitychange — re-fetch when tab becomes visible, (2) IntersectionObserver — re-fetch when slot list enters viewport, (3) user interaction — re-fetch on duration change or slot tap. Enforces minimum 15-30 second cooldown between fetches. Returns a refresh function that SlotGenerationUtility can respond to.

title: Create useSlotPresence hook (Supabase Realtime)
description: Hook that uses Supabase Realtime Presence to show how many people are viewing each time slot. On mount: join presence channel for the event. Broadcasts which slot the user is currently viewing/hovering. Aggregates all presence state into a map of slot → viewer count. Returns presenceData for TimeList to consume. Supabase free tier allows 200 concurrent connections — plenty for events.

title: Create /display page route
description: New app/display/page.tsx that composes everything. Server side: createPageConfiguration with overrides for event durations [5,10,15,20,25,30] and today-only date range. Client side: DurationPicker (no price), TimeList (with presence badges), EventBookingForm. Wires up useSmartRefresh and useSlotPresence hooks. Mobile-first layout, no date picker, no calendar. force-dynamic for fresh data.

title: Visual documentation of project state at each significant commit batch
description: Create screenshots/visual documentation showing the state of the project at each major milestone - what the site looked like at each significant batch of commits throughout the project history. Background task for historical record.

title: Implement hybrid slug configuration system (local + Supabase)
description:
Architecture: Add Supabase-backed slug configs that override local ones

GOAL: Enable live promo management without redeployment

CURRENT: All ~20 slug configs hardcoded in fetchSlugConfigurationData.ts

PROPOSED: Hybrid system
- Local configs (version controlled, stable)
- Supabase configs (live editable, temporary promos)
- Supabase wins on conflict

DATABASE SCHEMA:
Table: public.slug_configurations
Fields: slug (unique), type, title, pricing, discount, location, etc.
RLS policies for admin access
Indexes on: slug, is_active, promo_end_date

IMPLEMENTATION PHASES:
1. Create DB migration & update fetchSlugConfigurationData() (Immediate)
2. Test in staging with ENABLE_SUPABASE_SLUGS flag
3. Verify override behavior works
4. Enable in production
5. Gradually migrate time-sensitive promos

BENEFITS:
- Live promo editing without redeploy
- A/B testing different copy/pricing
- Temporary override of stable configs
- Admin UI for promo management

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Merge configs correctly (Supabase wins)
- All existing tests pass
- Graceful fallback when DB unavailable
- Can create Supabase slug that overrides local

title: Adopt release versioning system
description:
Move from commit-based monthly changelog tracking to versioned releases with proper tags and automated changelogs.

RESEARCH SUMMARY
================

Current state: package.json has version 0.2.0, changelog page tracks monthly commits manually in data/changelog/*.list.ts files. Commit messages already follow conventional-commits (feat/fix/chore/refactor format).

VERSIONING SCHEME OPTIONS
-------------------------

1) SemVer (e.g. 1.2.3) — Major.Minor.Patch
   For web apps: Major = big redesign or breaking workflow change, Minor = new user-facing feature, Patch = bugfix or tweak.
   Pros: Industry standard, tooling support everywhere.
   Cons: 'breaking change' is fuzzy for apps (no API consumers). Version numbers feel arbitrary.

2) CalVer (e.g. 2026.02, 2026.02.1)
   Pros: Version instantly tells you when it shipped. Great for apps with continuous delivery. No debates about major vs minor.
   Cons: Less tooling support. Doesn't communicate scope of change. Some tools (npm, GitHub) expect semver-ish format.

3) Hybrid CalVer+patch (e.g. 2026.2.0, 2026.2.1)
   Combines time context with patch numbering. Used by pip, Ubuntu, Unity.

RECOMMENDATION: For a solo-dev web app, CalVer is appealing for simplicity but semver has better tooling support. Either works — the real value is in having tagged releases at all.

TOOLING OPTIONS (see child issues)
-----------------------------------
- release-please (Google) — best fit for conventional-commits workflow
- release-it — lightweight CLI, good for solo devs
- Manual git tags + GitHub releases — zero dependencies, full control

WHAT THIS EPIC COVERS
---------------------
- Choose versioning scheme
- Choose tooling (or manual approach)
- Set up automation
- Migrate changelog page to pull from releases instead of manual commit lists
- Tag a v1.0.0 (or 2026.2) as the starting point

title: Option A: release-please (Google) — automated release PRs
description:
release-please reads conventional commit messages and auto-generates a Release PR on GitHub. When you merge that PR, it creates a git tag, GitHub release, and updates CHANGELOG.md.

HOW IT WORKS
============
1. GitHub Action runs on every push to main
2. Parses commit messages (feat → minor bump, fix → patch, breaking → major)
3. Maintains an open Release PR that accumulates changes
4. You merge the PR when ready to cut a release
5. Creates git tag + GitHub release with generated changelog

PROS
====
- Already uses conventional commits (project is set up for this)
- Zero local tooling needed — runs entirely in GitHub Actions
- Release PR gives you a review gate before cutting a release
- Auto-generates structured changelog from commits
- Supports both semver and custom versioning
- Maintained by Google, active development
- Works for non-npm projects (uses release-please-config.json + manifest)

CONS
====
- GitHub-only (not portable to other forges)
- Config can be finicky (release-please-config.json + manifest file)
- The Release PR pattern means an extra merge step
- v4 had some migration pain; docs can be confusing
- Opinionated about commit message format (but we already match)
- Overkill if you just want simple tags

EFFORT: ~1-2 hours to set up GitHub Action + config files
COMPLEXITY: Medium — some config to get right initially, then hands-off

VERDICT: Best fit if you want automation with a review gate. The project already writes conventional commits, so release-please would work out of the box. Main downside is it adds CI complexity for a solo dev project.

title: Option B: release-it — lightweight CLI for versioning + tags
description:
release-it is a CLI tool you run locally (or in CI) that bumps version, creates git tag, generates changelog, and optionally creates a GitHub release — all in one command.

HOW IT WORKS
============
1. Run 'pnpm release-it' (or 'pnpm release-it patch/minor/major')
2. Interactive prompts: confirm version bump, review changelog, confirm tag
3. Bumps package.json version, commits, tags, pushes
4. Creates GitHub release with changelog
5. With @release-it/conventional-changelog plugin, auto-determines bump from commits

PROS
====
- Simple: one command to release
- Interactive by default — shows you what it will do, asks for confirmation
- --dry-run mode to preview without side effects
- Works with conventional commits via plugin, or manual version choice
- Not tied to GitHub (works with GitLab, Bitbucket)
- Lightweight — just a dev dependency + small config in package.json
- Can generate CHANGELOG.md file
- No CI/GitHub Actions required (though can be added)

CONS
====
- Requires running a command locally (extra step vs fully automated)
- Plugin ecosystem smaller than release-please
- Still need to remember to run it (no automatic PR reminder)
- Changelog generation requires the conventional-changelog plugin (extra dep)

EFFORT: ~30 minutes to install + configure
COMPLEXITY: Low — add devDependency, add release config to package.json

SETUP SKETCH
============
pnpm add -D release-it @release-it/conventional-changelog
# Add to package.json:
# 'release-it': { 'plugins': { '@release-it/conventional-changelog': { 'preset': 'angular', 'infile': 'CHANGELOG.md' } } }
# Add script: 'release': 'release-it'

VERDICT: Best fit for solo dev who wants control + simplicity. One command, clear prompts, no CI setup required. Can graduate to CI automation later. Lowest barrier to entry.

title: Option C: Manual git tags + GitHub releases — zero dependencies
description:
The simplest approach: manually create git tags and GitHub releases when you want to mark a version. No tooling, no dependencies, no automation.

HOW IT WORKS
============
1. Decide it is time for a release
2. Update version in package.json (optional)
3. git tag -a v1.1.0 -m 'feat: booking reschedule, reminder system'
4. git push origin v1.1.0
5. Create GitHub release from the tag (gh release create v1.1.0 --generate-notes)
6. GitHub auto-generates release notes from commits since last tag

PROS
====
- Zero dependencies, zero config, zero CI
- Full control over when and what to release
- GitHub's --generate-notes flag auto-creates decent release notes from PRs/commits
- Nothing to maintain or update
- Works right now with no setup
- Can always add automation later
- gh CLI makes it a one-liner

CONS
====
- Manual process — easy to forget or skip releases
- No structured CHANGELOG.md generation (only GitHub release notes)
- Version in package.json can drift from tags if not disciplined
- No review gate or automation safety net
- Release notes quality depends on commit message quality (which is already good here)
- Doesn't scale if release frequency increases

EFFORT: ~5 minutes (just start tagging)
COMPLEXITY: None

PRACTICAL WORKFLOW
==================
# After a batch of work:
gh release create v1.1.0 --generate-notes --title 'v1.1.0: Booking Reschedule + Reminders'
# That is it. GitHub creates the tag, release, and auto-generated notes.

VERDICT: Best fit if you want versioning with absolute minimum overhead. The project already has good commit messages, so GitHub's auto-generated notes will be decent. Can be the starting point and graduate to release-it or release-please when/if the manual process becomes friction.

title: useSmartRefresh: dead import + mixed concerns

title: showPriceProps naming is redundant

title: White-label: FAQ and remaining content from siteConfig

title: useSmartRefresh: click handler fires fetch on every click
description: document.addEventListener('click') triggers a fetch on ANY click anywhere on the page. Should be scoped to meaningful interactions or removed entirely — visibility change + interval + touchstart is enough.
